SFTD.Warning: Be aware, this will clear all Heat/Wanted
SFTD.CrewTypeBox: Click to choose a Crew Type
SFTD.ChooseOneOrTwoEdges: Choose one or two edges and an equal number of flaws
SFTD.AddAdditionalTypeOfGangText:
You can add an additional type to a gang or expert by spending two crew upgrades.
When a cohort performs actions for which its types apply, it uses its full quality rating. Otherwise, its quality is zero. A given cohort can have up to two types. Some crew upgrades will add the “Elite” feature to a gang, which gives them +1d when they roll for a given Type.
SFTD.ClassExpClue1: Every time you roll a desperate action, mark xp in that action's attribute.
SFTD.ClassExpClue2: You expressed your beliefs, drives, heritage, or background.
SFTD.ClassExpClue3: You struggled with issues from your vice or traumas during the session.
SFTD.ClassExpClueDescription: At the end of each session, for each item below, mark 1 xp (in your playbook or an attribute) or 2 xp if that item occurred multiple times.
SFTD.CrewExpClue1: At the end of each session, for each item below, mark 1 xp (or instead mark 2xp if that item occurred multiple times).
SFTD.GangTypeRoversDescription: Sailors, carriage drivers, and deathlands scavengers.
SFTD.EdgesFearsomeDescription: The cohort is terrifying in aspect and reputation.
SFTD.EdgesIndependentDescription: The cohort can be trusted to make good decisions and act on their own initiative in the absence of direct orders.
SFTD.EdgesLoyalDescription: The cohort can't be bribed or turned against you.
SFTD.EdgesTenaciousDescription: The cohort won't be deterred from a task.
SFTD.FlawsPrincipledDescription: The cohort has an ethic or values that it won't betray.
SFTD.FlawsSavageDescription: The cohort is excessively violent and cruel.
SFTD.FlawsUnreliableDescription: The cohort isn't always available, due to other obligations, stupefaction from their vices, etc.
SFTD.FlawsWildDescription: The cohort is drunken, debauched, and loud-mouthed.
SFTD.HarmImpairedDescription: The cohort operates with reduced quality (-1d).
SFTD.HarmBrokenDescription: The cohort can't do anything until they recover.
SFTD.HarmDeadDescription: The cohort is destroyed. You can spend coin equal to your Tier +2 to restore it, plus two downtime activities to recruit new gang members, or hire a new expert.
SFTD.RollPartialSuccessPositionControlled: You hesitate. Withdraw and try a different approach, or else do it with a minor consequence: a minor complication occurs, you have reduced effect, you suffer lesser harm, you end up in a risky position.
SFTD.RollPartialSuccessPositionRisky: You do it, but there's a consequence: you suffer harm, a complication occurs, you have reduced effect, you end up in a desperate position.
SFTD.RollPartialSuccessPosition: You do it, but there's a consequence: you suffer severe harm, a serious complication occurs, you have reduced effect.
SFTD.RollFailurePositionControlled: You falter. Press on by seizing a risky opportunity, or withdraw and try a different approach.
SFTD.RollFailurePositionRisky: Things go badly. You suffer harm, a complication occurs, you end up in a desperate position, you lose this opportunity.
SFTD.RollFailurePositionDesperate: It's the worst outcome. You suffer severe harm, a serious complication occurs, you lose this opportunity for action.
SFTD.RollResistance:
You reduce or avoid the effects of the consequence (GM chooses).
Suffer {stress} stress.
SFTD.RollResistanceCritical:
You reduce or avoid the effects of the consequence (GM chooses).
Clear 1 stress.
SFTD.RollThreatPeril: Suffer a reduced consequence or push yourself to avoid the threat.
SFTD.RollThreatThreat: You suffer the consequence If you push yourself, reduce the consequence.
SFTD.EffectWarning: Managing Active Effects within an Owned Item is not currently supported by FoundryVTT and will be added in a subsequent update.
SFTD.Name: Name
SFTD.AcquireAssetExtra: To raise the result beyond critical, you may spend 2 coin per +1 Tier bonus.
SFTD.RollViceFailure: You overindulge. Clear {clear_stress} stress and choose one of the following consequences:
SFTD.RollViceFailureAttactTrouble: Attract Trouble. Select or roll an additional entanglement.
SFTD.RollViceFailureLost: Lost. Your character vanishes for a few weeks. Play a different character until this one returns from their bender. When your character returns, they've also healed any harm they had.
SFTD.RollViceFailureTapped: Tapped. Your current purveyor cuts you off. Find a new source for your vice.
SFTD.RollFortuneCritical: The result is exceptional. You have great or extreme effect.
SFTD.RollFortuneSuccess: The result is good. You have standard or full effect.
SFTD.RollFortunePartialSuccess: The result is mixed. You have limited or partial effect.
SFTD.RollFortuneFailure: The result is bad. You have poor or little effect.
SFTD.RollGatherInformationSuccess: Great: You get exceptional details. The information is complete and follow-up questions may expand into related areas or reveal more than you hoped for.
SFTD.RollGatherInformationPartialSuccess: Standard: You get good details. Clarifying and follow-up questions are possible.
SFTD.RollGatherInformationFailure: Limited: You get incomplete or partial information. More information gathering will be needed to get all the answers.
SFTD.RollEngagementCritical: Exceptional result. You've already overcome the first obstacle and you're in a controlled position for what's next.
SFTD.RollEngagementSuccess: Good result. You're in a controlled position when the action starts.
SFTD.RollEngagementPartialSuccess: Mixed result. You're in a risky position when the action starts.
SFTD.RollEngagementFailure: Bad result. You're in a desperate position when the action starts.
SFTD.RollAcquireAssetResult: You gain an asset of Tier {tier_quality} quality.
SFTD.DisabledByAE: Minimum Value set by an Active Effect

Action.Name: Action Rolls
Action.Hint: Enables the Action Roll from the Actor Dice Roller; only uncheck if you have enabled Threat Rolls.

Threat.Name: Threat Rolls
Threat.Hint: Enables the Threat Roll from the Deep Cuts supplement.

Push.Name: Push Yourself Rolls
Push.Hint: Replaces the base Resistance Roll with the Push Yourself Roll from the Deep Cuts supplement.

Load.Name: Alternate Load
Load.Hint: Replaces the base Load Rules (Light/Normal/Heavy) with the Load Rules (Discreet/Conspicuous) from the Deep Cuts supplement.

ClockXP.Name: Experience Clocks
ClockXP.Hint: Replaces the base Experience Rules with Experience Clocks from the Deep Cuts supplement.

Edge.Name: Edge
Edge.Hint: Adds Edge to character sheets from the Deep Cuts supplement.

PublicClocks.Name: Default Observable Clocks
PublicClocks.Hint: All newly made Clock actors will have permissions set to Observer for all players.

info.SameNPC: The dropped NPC is already an acquaintance of this character.
info.SameCrew: The dropped Crew is the current crew of this character.

warn.NoNPC: This NPC does not exist in the World or in a Compendium.
